---
title: "Scientific writing websites"
id: activity-gis-reproducible-research-blogs
order: 300
summary: "Online blogs for deploying studies and more."
modules:
  - gis-reproducible-research
Goals: []
materials:
  - material-gis-reproducible-research-intro
  - material-guide
assessments: []
labels:
  - basic
  - recommended
published: true
source: "../LV-uav-workflow/morea/gis-reproducible-research/activity-gis-reproducible-research-blogs.md"
---

# Blogging portals for project reports and more

## GitHub related

GitHub-based approaches are useful because they are free of charge, support version control, can use automated workflows, and integrate well with IT-related work.

Options include:

- [fastpages](https://github.com/fastai/fastpages), which can integrate Word `.docx` documents.
- `R`-focused blogdown and Hugo workflows, described in [Academic writing made easy](https://gisma-courses.github.io/gi-modules/post/2021-11-16-making-of-academic-websites/).
- The [fast-template](https://www.fast.ai/2020/01/16/fast_template/) package.

## Non-GitHub related

If you are not familiar with GitHub, free blogging portals can also be used:

- [hpage](https://de.hpage.com/)
- [wix](https://www.wix.com/)
- [netlify](https://www.netlify.com/pricing/)
- [blogger](https://www.blogger.com/)

## Connected Module

- [Reproducible Research](../modules/gis-reproducible-research.qmd)
